body: ### Problem The `detail` value of the [CompletionItem](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#completionItem) is never shown in the complete popup. ### Steps to reproduce using "nvim --clean -u minimal_init.lua" Minimal init example for typescript-language-server: ```lua vim.opt.completeopt = { 'menuone', 'noinsert', 'noselect', 'popup' } local pattern = 'typescript' local cmd = { 'typescript-language-server', '--stdio' } -- Add files/folders here that indicate the root of a project local root_markers = { '.git', '.editorconfig' } -- Change to table with settings if required local settings = vim.empty_dict() vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('FileType', { pattern = pattern, callback = function(args) local match = vim.fs.find(root_markers, { path = args.file, upward = true })[1] local root_dir = match and vim.fn.fnamemodify(match, ':p:h') or nil vim.lsp.start({ name = 'ts_ls', cmd = cmd, root_dir = root_dir, settings = settings, }) end, }) vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('LspAttach', { callback = function(ev) vim.lsp.completion.enable(true, ev.data.client_id, ev.buf, { autotrigger = true, }) end, }) -- Uncomment lines below to see example with details -- local function on_complete_changed() -- local completed_item = vim.api.nvim_get_vvar('completed_item') -- if not completed_item or not completed_item.user_data or not completed_item.user_data.nvim then -- return -- end -- local completion_item = completed_item.user_data.nvim.lsp.completion_item --- @type lsp.CompletionItem -- local client_id = completed_item.user_data.nvim.lsp.client_id --- @type integer -- local client = vim.lsp.get_client_by_id(client_id) -- if not client then -- return -- end -- -- client:request(vim.lsp.protocol.Methods.completionItem_resolve, completion_item, function(err, result) -- if err or not result then -- return -- end -- -- local text = {} -- -- if result and result.documentation then -- local docs = type(result.documentation) == 'string' and result.documentation or result.documentation.value -- vim.list_extend(text, vim.split(docs, '\n')) -- end -- -- if result.detail and not vim.startswith(text[1] or '', '```') then -- text = vim.list_extend({ '```' .. vim.bo.filetype, result.detail, '```' }, text) -- end -- -- if #text == 0 then -- return -- end -- -- local _, popup_winid = vim.lsp.util.open_floating_preview(text, 'markdown', { -- border = 'single', -- }) -- local pos = vim.fn.pum_getpos() -- if not pos or not pos.row or not pos.col then -- return -- end -- vim.api.nvim_win_set_config(popup_winid, { -- relative = 'editor', -- row = pos.row, -- col = pos.col + pos.width + (pos.scrollbar and 1 or 0), -- }) -- end) -- end -- -- vim.opt.completeopt:remove('popup') -- vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('CompleteChanged', { -- callback = function() -- on_complete_changed() -- end, -- }) -- ``` 1. Open some typescript file, for example `nvim test.ts` 2. Try typing in `Math.m`, you should get max and min in the autocomplete 3. When the documentation appears, it will miss `detail` part 4. Uncomment the lines in the minimal init 5. Repeat steps 1-3, documentation will have detail value Screenshots: Without details (current Neovim behavior): With details: ### Expected behavior Expected to see `detail` value from the `completionItem/resolve` response since it provides valuable information, like method description, import information (when importing from other file), etc. I'm not sure if my solution in the minimal init should be used, but that worked for me, and I think I copied it from somewhere some time ago (blink.cmp or somewhere else). I also don't know how other language servers provide this information and that's why I'm not attempting to add this myself. ### Nvim version (nvim -v) NVIM v0.13.0-dev-110+g8603fc9180 ### Language server name/version 5.1.3 ### Operating system/version Arch Linux ### Log file _No response_

comment_data.body:
The spec doesn't prescribe how or where clients should render these, that's intentionally left to the client  related https://github.com/microsoft/language-server-protocol/issues/1115.

The detail field carries valuable information (method signatures, type info, import paths), so it makes sense to surface it in an info popup.

title: LSP: Completion item resolve does not show documentation details

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
